data.title: Contractor Foreman: Step-by-Step Construction Management
data.excerpt: Learn how to manage budgets, projects, work orders, and invoicing with Contractor Foreman. Follow this tutorial and start your free trial today!

children.text: *Disclosure: Some links in this post are affiliate links. If you sign up through them, I may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you.*

If you run a contracting business, you already know the chaos: sticky notes with change orders, spreadsheets that never balance, text threads with crews that get buried under a hundred other messages. Contractor Foreman replaces all of that with one platform that actually makes sense for the way contractors work — not the way software engineers think contractors should work.
This step-by-step tutorial walks you through exactly how to use
Contractor Foreman
from your very first login to sending invoices that get you paid. No fluff, no theory — just the practical steps you need to take control of your business today.
What Is Contractor Foreman?
Contractor Foreman

is an all-in-one construction management platform built specifically for general contractors, subcontractors, and specialty trade professionals. It covers project management, estimating, budgeting, work orders, time tracking, invoicing, and document storage — all starting at $49 per month.

What makes it different from generic project management tools? It was designed by people who understand construction workflows. You won't find unnecessary features built for tech startups. Instead, you get tools that solve real problems: tracking job costs against estimates, managing crew schedules, handling change orders without losing your mind, and generating professional invoices without hiring a bookkeeper.

Capterra users rate it 4.5 out of 5, and the most common praise you'll see is about its ease of use. That matters when your foreman needs to log hours from a job site and your office manager needs to pull a budget report — neither of them should need a computer science degree.
If you want to dive deeper into what the platform offers, check out our overview on
what is Contractor Foreman
Step 1: Create Your Account and Add Your First Project
Getting started takes about five minutes. Head to the
Contractor Foreman sign-up page

, choose a plan, and create your account. You can start with the Basic plan at $49/month, which includes unlimited storage, apps for phone and tablet, and a web panel. There's a 100-day money-back guarantee, so you have plenty of runway to test everything.

Once you're inside the dashboard, your first move is creating a project. Click "Projects" in the sidebar and hit "Add New." You'll enter the project name, client information, address, start date, and projected completion date. Contractor Foreman lets you assign a project manager and tag the project with categories (residential, commercial, renovation, etc.) so everything stays organized as your portfolio grows.

One feature I genuinely appreciate: you can attach documents, permits, blueprints, and photos directly to the project file. No more digging through email attachments or a shared drive with a naming convention nobody follows. Everything lives in one place, tied to the project it belongs to.
Pro tip: set up your client database first if you have repeat customers. It saves time when creating new projects and keeps all communication history linked to the right contact.
For more setup strategies, our guide on
Contractor Foreman tips and best practices
covers the workflows that experienced users rely on.
Step 2: Build Accurate Estimates and Set Budgets

This is where Contractor Foreman earns its keep. Open any project and navigate to the "Estimates" section. You can build estimates line by line, adding labor costs, material costs, equipment, and subcontractor expenses. The interface lets you create templates for recurring job types — so if you do kitchen remodels regularly, you can load a template and adjust quantities instead of starting from scratch every time.

Each line item can include unit costs, quantities, markup percentages, and notes. As you add items, the running total updates in real time. You can generate a professional estimate PDF and email it directly to your client for approval. When they approve it, that estimate becomes your project budget baseline.

Here's the part that separates Contractor Foreman from spreadsheets: every actual expense you log gets compared against the estimate automatically. You see variance in real time. If your framing materials come in 12% over budget, you know immediately — not when the project is finished and the money is gone.

The budget tracking extends to change orders too. When a client requests a scope change, you create a change order within the project, get approval, and the budget adjusts. No separate documents, no confusion about what was original scope versus additions.
Curious how this stacks up for different types of businesses? Explore our article on
Contractor Foreman use cases
to see how companies across trades are using these features.
Step 3: Manage Work Orders and Field Crews

With your project and budget set up, it's time to manage the actual work. Contractor Foreman's work order system lets you break a project into tasks, assign them to specific crew members or subcontractors, set due dates, and track completion status.

Create a work order by going to the "Work Orders" tab within a project. Specify the scope of work, assign the responsible party, and attach any relevant documents — drawings, material specs, safety protocols, whatever the crew needs. Your field team sees these assignments on their mobile app, marks progress, uploads photos of completed work, and flags issues in real time.

The GPS-enabled time cards deserve special mention. When a crew member clocks in through the app, the system logs their location. This isn't about surveillance — it's about accurate job costing. You know exactly which hours belong to which project, and your payroll data is clean without anyone manually transferring timesheets.

Daily logs are another powerful tool. Supervisors can record weather conditions, visitor information, deliveries, equipment on site, safety incidents, and general notes. These logs become invaluable if you ever need to document delays or disputes. Everything is timestamped and tied to the project.
If you're weighing different software options for team and task coordination, our comparison of
how to use monday.com
and
how to use ClickUp
gives you the context for understanding what makes construction-specific tools like Contractor Foreman a smarter fit for the industry.
Step 4: Track Time, Expenses, and Job Progress

Accurate tracking is the difference between profitable projects and ones that quietly bleed money. Contractor Foreman gives you multiple ways to capture this data without creating extra work for your team.

Time tracking happens through the mobile app. Crew members tap in and out, and the hours flow directly into the project's labor costs. Supervisors can review and approve timesheets before they hit payroll. No more collecting paper timesheets on Friday and trying to decipher handwriting.

Expense tracking works similarly. Upload receipts by photo, categorize them (materials, equipment rental, permits, subcontractor payments), and assign them to a project. The system totals everything against your budget so you always know where you stand.

The project dashboard gives you a visual summary: percentage complete, budget consumed, upcoming deadlines, and open issues. You can generate reports by project, by client, by date range, or by expense category. These reports export to PDF or CSV, making it straightforward to share updates with stakeholders or your accountant.
A feature that consistently earns praise in
Capterra reviews

is the reporting depth. You're not just seeing that a project is 60% done — you're seeing that framing is at 85%, plumbing is at 40%, and electrical hasn't started yet. That granularity helps you make informed decisions about resource allocation.
Step 5: Generate Invoices and Collect Payments

You've done the work, tracked the costs, and the project is wrapping up. Now get paid. Contractor Foreman's invoicing module pulls from your project data — estimates, change orders, and logged expenses — to create accurate invoices without manual re-entry.

Navigate to "Invoices" within your project, select the billing method (progress billing, milestone billing, or final invoice), and the system populates the line items. Add any additional charges, apply retainage if your contract includes it, and send the invoice directly to your client via email.

Online payment acceptance is built in. Your clients can pay through the invoice link, and payments are recorded against the project automatically. This eliminates the "I sent a check last week" conversation because everything is visible and timestamped.
The
Contractor Foreman platform

also handles A/R aging reports, so you can see at a glance which invoices are outstanding, which are overdue, and which clients need a friendly nudge. Cash flow management is one of the biggest pain points for contractors, and having this visibility makes a real difference.
Want to explore the newest capabilities? Our breakdown of
Contractor Foreman's new features
covers the latest updates the development team has rolled out.
Standout Features That Make Contractor Foreman Shine
Beyond the core workflow above, several features push Contractor Foreman into "why didn't I switch sooner" territory:
Unlimited Storage
: Upload as many photos, documents, blueprints, and videos as you need. No paying extra for storage tiers.
No Device Limit
: Your whole team can access the platform from their phones, tablets, and computers simultaneously without per-device fees.
Price-Lock Guarantee
: The price you sign up at is the price you keep. No surprise increases at renewal.
Free Training and Support
: Onboarding help, video tutorials, and responsive support are included at every plan level.
35+ Integrated Features
: From proposal generation to safety management to customer portals, the platform keeps expanding without bloating.
Subcontractor Management
: Issue work orders to subs, track their progress, and manage their payments within the same system.
Client Portal
: Give your clients a branded view of their project's progress, approved change orders, and invoices. Transparency builds trust.

These aren't throwaway extras. Each one addresses a real operational pain point that contractors face daily. The fact that they're all included in a platform starting at $49/month is genuinely remarkable in this market.
Who Is Contractor Foreman Ideal For?
Contractor Foreman is built for construction professionals who need professional-grade tools without enterprise-level complexity or cost. Specifically, it's a perfect fit for:
General Contractors
managing multiple projects simultaneously and needing visibility across their entire portfolio.
Subcontractors
(electricians, plumbers, HVAC techs, roofers) who want clean job costing and fast invoicing without overkill features they'll never touch.
Small to Mid-Size Construction Companies
(2-100 employees) that have outgrown spreadsheets but don't want to spend six figures on enterprise software.
Specialty Trade Professionals
like landscapers, painters, concrete contractors, and remodelers who need estimating and project tracking tailored to their workflow.
Solo Contractors
who wear every hat and need a system that automates the administrative burden so they can focus on the work.
If you've tried generic tools like
Apollo.io
for client management or broader platforms for task tracking and found them lacking for construction-specific needs, Contractor Foreman is the answer. It speaks your language from the first click.
Ready to see it in action? **
Start your free trial of Contractor Foreman

** and experience the difference a purpose-built platform makes. With a 100-day money-back guarantee and plans starting at $49/month, there's essentially no risk. Your future self — the one who's not drowning in paperwork — will thank you.

children.text: How much does Contractor Foreman cost?

Contractor Foreman starts at $49/month for the Basic plan, which includes unlimited storage, mobile and web access, and no device limits. Higher-tier plans with additional features are available, and all plans come with a 100-day money-back guarantee.
Can I use Contractor Foreman on my phone at the job site?

Yes. Contractor Foreman offers mobile apps for both iOS and Android alongside its web panel. Field crews can clock in with GPS-enabled time cards, view work orders, upload progress photos, and submit daily logs — all from their phones or tablets.
Is Contractor Foreman suitable for solo contractors or only large teams?

It works for both. Solo contractors benefit from streamlined estimating, invoicing, and project tracking without paying for unused team features. Larger crews take advantage of subcontractor management, multi-user access, and the client portal. The pricing and feature set scale well across business sizes.
